dialogue_line_replacements.text: Muchas gracias. Es un placer estar aquí. Estoy sumamente interesado en esta posición.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: Thank you very much. It's a pleasure to be here. I'm extremely interested in this position. (PR: posición common in bilingual context)

dialogue_line_replacements.text: Con mucho gusto. Logré aumentar las ventas en un veinte por ciento durante mi gestión.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: With pleasure. I managed to increase sales by twenty percent during my tenure.

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: Puerto Rico has a strong bilingual professional culture. English business terms often appear in Spanish speech: 'el networking', 'el follow-up', 'el deadline'. Formal register still uses usted but code-switching is common.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: In PR professional settings, 'la posición' (position) is used alongside 'el puesto'. 'El résumé' or 'el currículo' for resume. Puerto Rican formal Spanish is influenced by US corporate culture.
